_meta.engine: JSONL — in-memory Map, full-file rewrite on every debounced flush

files.name: repos.jsonl

field_schema.name: string
field_schema.tags: string[] (optional, referenced in repos/route.ts filter path)

files.first_ts: n/a (no embedded ts; order is insertion)
files.daily_growth_est: bounded — one row per tracked repo. Grows only when seed list expands.
read_by: all /api/** routes reach repoStore.getAll()/get() via in-memory Map — file is NOT re-read per request
files.pruning: none — upsert-by-id; tombstoned via repo.deleted=true but rows stay
files.scan_cost_now: hydrate: 1 readFile + 309 JSON.parse (~0.3MB). Queries: O(1) Map.get, O(n) Array.from.
files.scan_cost_x100: 30,000 repos => ~30MB JSONL, full rewrite on every debounced persist. readFile+parse spike on cold start ~1-3s. Queries stay O(n) over memory Map.

files.name: snapshots.jsonl

field_schema.id: string (composite: '${repoId}:${capturedAt}:${source}' — legacy '${repoId}:${capturedAt}' is auto-migrated on hydrate, see memory-stores.ts:256)

files.daily_growth_est: ~10 rows/day currently (304 lines / 30 days). At 309 repos with daily cadence that would be 309/day; the retention cap at 120 per repo + the SNAPSHOT_HISTORY_CAP slicing hides the real ingest volume. Realistic steady state = (repo_count * cadence_per_day) with cap enforced in memory, so on-disk count is bounded by repo_count * 120.
written_by: src/lib/pipeline/storage/memory-stores.ts:236 InMemorySnapshotStore.persist() — full rewrite of flat list
files.pruning: SNAPSHOT_HISTORY_CAP = 120 per repo (~30 days at 6h cadence). Enforced in-memory on append — oldest slice dropped. File mirrors memory on next debounced persist.
files.scan_cost_now: 304 lines, sub-millisecond parse. append() does filter+sort+slice O(n log n) per call where n<=120.
files.scan_cost_x100: 30,000 repos * 120 cap = 3.6M lines * ~400B = ~1.4GB JSONL on disk. Every mutation triggers debounced persist = full 1.4GB rewrite within 2s. readFileSync-style hydrate will OOM on a 512MB Railway dyno around 1-2M lines.

files.name: scores.jsonl

field_schema.components: object { starVelocity24h, starVelocity7d, forkVelocity7d, contributorGrowth30d, commitFreshness, releaseFreshness, socialBuzz, issueActivity, communityHealth, categoryMomentum } — all numbers

files.daily_growth_est: bounded — one row per repo, upsert by repoId. File size scales only with repo count.
files.scan_cost_now: 309 rows, ~0.3MB rewrite on persist. All 9 timestamps above cluster in a 10ms window = single recompute pass.
files.scan_cost_x100: 30k rows * ~900B = ~27MB. Full rewrite every debounced cycle. Acceptable on SSD but blocks event loop briefly on rewrite under Node.

field_schema.primary: object { categoryId, confidence(number), matched { topics[], keywords[], ownerPrefix|null } }
field_schema.secondary: array of same shape as primary

files.name: reasons.jsonl

field_schema.details: array of { code, headline, detail, confidence(low|med|high), timeframe, evidence:[{label,value}] }

files.pruning: none — upsert-by-repoId; overwrite on each recompute

files.name: mentions.jsonl
files.daily_growth_est: UNBOUNDED once social ingest is wired. Append-only, NO retention cap in memory-stores.ts.
files.pruning: NONE — this is the sleeping scale bomb. No per-repo cap, no age cutoff.
files.scan_cost_x100: At 30k repos * avg 20 mentions/day * 365 days = 219M rows * ~500B = ~110GB. File-based storage fails long before that — full rewrite on every append-debounce becomes impossible past ~1GB.

files.name: alert-rules.jsonl
files.daily_growth_est: bounded by user count * rules-per-user (user feature not live yet)

files.name: alert-events.jsonl
files.daily_growth_est: append-only log. Scales with (rules * cross-threshold events/day).
files.pruning: NONE — append-only, no retention policy. Secondary scale bomb after mentions.
files.scan_cost_x100: Unbounded — will grow linearly with time once alerting ships.

concurrency_model.write_strategy: full-file atomic rewrite — tmp-sibling write then rename (file-persistence.ts:134 writeJsonlFile). The canonical filename never contains a partial buffer.
concurrency_model.debounce: PERSIST_DEBOUNCE_MS = 2000. Every store mutator fires the 'onStoreMutation' hook (memory-stores.ts:57) which calls schedulePersist() (singleton.ts:132) — a leading-edge debounce: timer resets on each mutation, so a burst of writes produces ONE flush 2s after the burst ends.
concurrency_model.locking: NONE — no file lock, no advisory lock, no fsync. Two concurrent Node processes writing the same .data/ would race on rename and can corrupt. Single-process protection only: singleton.ts holds a module-level persistTimer, and ingest.ts:162 holds a module-level 'batchInFlight' mutex to serialize ingest batches.
concurrency_model.dedup: per-store: InMemorySnapshotStore.append() filters by s.id before push (memory-stores.ts:183). InMemoryRepoStore.upsert handles GitHub rename collisions (memory-stores.ts:102). No cross-file consistency.
concurrency_model.corruption_vector_on_serverless: writeJsonlFile serializes the entire in-memory Map on every flush, so two Vercel lambda instances each holding their own in-memory copy would clobber each other on rename. This is why the team redirected all traffic to the Railway single-process deployment (see ephemeral_disk_handling).

ephemeral_disk_handling.problem: Vercel serverless functions run on isolated containers with ephemeral /tmp that does not survive cold start. JSONL persistence requires a single long-lived filesystem.
ephemeral_disk_handling.current_fix: vercel.json is a PURE-REDIRECT config: '/' and '/:path+' both 302 to 'https://starscreener-production.up.railway.app/'. Vercel never serves dynamic traffic — it's a DNS/redirect shell. The Next.js app runs as a single long-lived Node process on Railway, where .data/ is a real persistent disk.
evidence: vercel.json:3-13 — the entire config is 2 redirect rules, no serverless functions, no rewrites
next.config.ts:13 — 'output: "standalone"' is commented out with note 'Uncomment for Docker/Railway/Fly deployments'
src/lib/pipeline/ingestion/ingest.ts:157-162 — explicit comment: 'The app runs as a single long-lived Node.js process (Railway / Fly / Render — Vercel serverless doesn't hold state anyway)'
src/app/api/stream/route.ts:10 and src/lib/pipeline/events.ts:64 — SSE noted as Railway-only

recent commits: b54ca49 'Rebuild: offset + skipRecompute', f76784b 'Restore Vercel -> Railway redirect: serverless /tmp can't hold pipeline state', 7e9f5da 'Add /api/pipeline/rebuild: real data engine for every seed repo'
ephemeral_disk_handling.persistence_boundary: Railway container filesystem, rooted at process.cwd()/.data (file-persistence.ts:37 currentDataDir). Environment can override with STARSCREENER_DATA_DIR. Railway mounts this as persistent storage tied to the instance.

backup_strategy.on_disk: .data/backup-2026-04-17/ exists with a snapshot of the JSONL files from 2026-04-17T13:04. Manual / ad-hoc — no rotation script found in src/.
backup_strategy.automatic: none — no cron route writing backups, no git-LFS of .data/, no S3 sync.
backup_strategy.risk: If Railway volume is lost or corrupted by a mid-rename crash between writeFile and rename (unlikely but possible), the single backup from 2026-04-17 is the only recovery point. Mentions/alert-events append-only streams have no recovery granularity at all.

read_path_analysis.cold_start: hydrateAll() in singleton.ts:72 runs readJsonlFile for all 9 files in parallel via Promise.all. Current cold start: ~1MB total, sub-100ms. The mutation hook is suspended during hydrate (singleton.ts:184 suspendPersistHook) so re-append during hydrate does NOT schedule a feedback-loop flush.
read_path_analysis.hot_path: NO file reads. Every API route calls pipeline.ensureReady() (which is memoized — readyPromise guard at pipeline.ts:121), then reads from the in-memory Map directly. 18 API route files hit repoStore/snapshotStore/etc; zero of them readFile.
read_path_analysis.scan_pattern: repoStore.getAll() returns Array.from(Map.values()) on every call — O(n) allocation. pipeline.getTopMovers() sorts that array per request. No query cache beyond HTTP cache headers (READ_CACHE_HEADERS).

schema_drift_risk.unknown_fields_on_read: TOLERATED — readJsonlFile<T>() uses JSON.parse without a zod/runtime schema check (file-persistence.ts:114). Extra fields flow into memory as-is; missing optional fields become 'undefined'. TypeScript type is a static claim only.
schema_drift_risk.missing_required_fields: SILENT CORRUPTION RISK — a missing 'repoId' on a snapshot would still hydrate into the Map under byRepo.get(undefined), leading to confusing lookups. No validation.
observed_migrations: Snapshot id shape migrated from '${repoId}:${capturedAt}' to '${repoId}:${capturedAt}:${source}' — auto-detected and rewritten on hydrate (memory-stores.ts:249-266), marks dirty so next persist flushes the new shape. Good pattern.
schema_drift_risk.malformed_lines: skipped with console.warn (file-persistence.ts:118). Blank lines tolerated. No line-count mismatch alarm.

scale_cliffs.failure: append-only with NO pruning. At 10x (3000 repos, social ingest enabled) and 20 mentions/repo/day this hits ~21M rows/year * 500B = ~10GB/year. Full-file rewrite on every debounced flush (writeJsonlFile, not appendJsonlFile — persist() is a full rewrite) blocks the event loop for multi-second windows and eventually exceeds disk.

30k repos * 120 cap = 3.6M lines * ~400B = ~1.4GB. Cold-start hydrate is readFile of the whole file + 3.6M JSON.parse calls — ~30-60s, likely OOM on 512MB container. Every debounced persist rewrites the entire 1.4GB. Break point is ~500k lines / ~200MB.

append-only with NO pruning, no TTL. At 30k repos and active alerting users, expected multiple events per repo per day; unbounded growth + full-rewrite persist means this breaks whenever total alert-event volume passes ~200MB (approx hundreds of thousands of events).

~30MB rewrite per flush. Blocks event loop ~200-400ms per write. Uncomfortable but survivable. The pain is in-memory: Array.from(byId.values()) in every getAll() call allocates 30k-element arrays per request.

recommendations: Ship the documented Postgres migration (docs/DATABASE.md). Descriptors already exist (src/lib/db/schema.ts), scaffold classes exist (src/lib/db/stores.ts). Gate on process.env.DATABASE_URL in singleton.ts so dev keeps JSONL.

FIRST TABLE TO MIGRATE: snapshots — it's the one with real time-series pressure, time-range query value, and the natural primary key (repo_id, captured_at) maps cleanly. Neon or Supabase with a monthly partition + 90d retention job.

Add retention policies to mentions and alert-events BEFORE social ingest + alerting ship. Mirror SNAPSHOT_HISTORY_CAP with a max-per-repo cap (e.g. 500 mentions/repo) or a rolling 30d cutoff, enforced in-memory on append.

Replace writeJsonlFile full-rewrite with a true appendJsonlFile path for append-only stores (snapshots, mentions, alert-events). helper already exists at file-persistence.ts:148 but is unused — stores call persist() which always rewrites everything.
Add runtime schema validation at the readJsonlFile boundary (zod or ajv). Current 'trust the type' approach is silent-corruption friendly.
Add fsync after rename in writeJsonlFile. Current atomic-rename is safe against partial writes but not against unflushed OS buffer loss on crash.
Treat /api/pipeline/persist + the debounce timer as a safety net only; once on Postgres, delete the JSONL path (STARSCREENER_PERSIST=false) and drop the 2s debounce.

When moving off Railway-single-instance, either pick a Postgres-backed stack OR a DO-style block volume + single-writer lock. Current code has ZERO multi-writer safety — two Railway replicas would corrupt each other.
